steps.title: Verify client exists in Syncro portal
steps.detail: Confirm the client organization exists with correct business name and contact info.

steps.title: Create asset folders and clone policies
steps.detail: Create Workstations and Servers folders. Clone SA template policies, rename for this client, and assign to the respective folders.

steps.title: Configure deployment scripts in cloned policies
steps.detail: Add required product deployment scripts for this client's products. Verify run order and conditions.

steps.title: Confirm RMM agent properties are populated
steps.detail: Navigate to client record → Properties. Confirm RMM Agent (Workstation) and/or RMM Agent (Server) fields are populated.

steps.title: Verify org auto-created in Threatlocker portal
steps.detail: After the first device enrolls, confirm the client org appears under your parent account with the correct name.

steps.title: Rename org if needed to match naming convention
steps.detail: If the auto-created org name does not match your standard format, rename it before more devices enroll.

steps.title: Note learning mode start and expected end date
steps.detail: Learning mode begins on first enrollment (~2 weeks). Document the end date and set a calendar reminder.

steps.title: Document known LOB applications for post-learning allowances
steps.detail: Record any critical apps or tools that will need to be explicitly allowed after learning mode ends.

steps.title: Verify org auto-created in Huntress portal after first device
steps.detail: After the first agent enrolls, confirm the client organization appears in the Huntress partner portal.

steps.title: Configure ITDR — connect Entra ID / M365 tenant
steps.detail: ITDR requires Global Admin consent for the client tenant. Configure via the Integrations section at account level, not the client org level.

steps.title: Configure SIEM log forwarding
steps.detail: If the client has a SIEM subscription, configure log forwarding destination.

steps.title: Review initial dashboard after first device enrollment
steps.detail: Check the Huntress dashboard for any immediate detections or flagged items. Investigate before proceeding.

steps.title: Create client organization in Duo Admin Panel
steps.detail: Log into the Duo Admin Panel. Create a new account/organization for this client.

steps.title: Configure Duo with Entra ID — EAM method (recommended)
steps.detail: The preferred method. Requires Entra ID P1/P2 licensing. Satisfies Microsoft mandatory MFA requirements.

substeps.title: Verify Entra ID P1/P2 licensing is assigned to all users
substeps.detail: Included in M365 E3, E5, F3, Business Premium. Must be added separately for lower-tier plans.

substeps.title: Create break-glass admin account excluded from Duo CA policies
substeps.detail: Create before making any CA changes. Store credentials securely in the client record.

substeps.title: Register Duo EAM application in Duo Admin Panel
substeps.detail: Applications → Protect an Application → Microsoft Entra ID: External Authentication Methods.

substeps.title: Authorize Duo Enterprise App in the client M365 tenant
substeps.detail: Click Authorize in Duo setup. Sign in as Global Admin to grant consent.

substeps.title: Register EAM in Entra ID Authentication Methods
substeps.detail: entra.microsoft.com → Security → Authentication Methods → Policies → External (Preview). Add Duo.

substeps.title: Create Conditional Access policy — Report-Only mode first
substeps.detail: Assign to pilot group. Target Office 365. Grant: Require MFA. Start in Report-Only.

substeps.title: Test with pilot user and switch CA policy to enforced
substeps.detail: Verify Duo prompt fires correctly, then expand to all users and switch to On.

steps.title: Fallback: Configure Duo with custom controls (legacy)
steps.detail: Use only if EAM fails or P1 licensing is unavailable. Does NOT satisfy Microsoft's mandatory MFA for Azure portals.

steps.title: Sync or import users into Duo
steps.detail: Configure Entra ID directory sync in the Duo Admin Panel, or perform a CSV import of user accounts.

steps.title: User enrollment tracking
steps.detail: Track which users have completed Duo enrollment.

freetext.label: Enrolled users (name / email — one per line)
freetext.placeholder:
John Smith — jsmith@client.com
Jane Doe — jdoe@client.com

steps.title: Create client enterprise node in Keeper Admin Console
steps.detail: Provision a new enterprise node. Set admin contact and SA delegate access.

steps.title: Configure SSO integration with Entra ID
steps.detail: Enables unified login — users authenticate through Entra ID (with Duo MFA) and access Keeper without a separate prompt.

substeps.title: Create Keeper Enterprise Application in Entra ID
substeps.detail: Entra admin center → Enterprise Applications → Create your own → Non-gallery. Name it Keeper Password Manager.

substeps.title: Configure SAML in Entra ID
substeps.detail: Open app → Single sign-on → SAML. You will need ACS URL and Entity ID from Keeper SSO Connect.

substeps.title: Configure SSO Connect in Keeper Admin Console
substeps.detail: Admin Console → SSO Connect → Add New. Select SAML 2.0. Copy ACS URL and Entity ID into Entra ID.

substeps.title: Upload Entra ID metadata to Keeper
substeps.detail: Download Federation Metadata XML from Entra ID. Upload into Keeper SSO Connect.

substeps.title: Test SSO login end-to-end — verify Duo fires in unified flow
substeps.detail: Log out of Keeper and log in via SSO. Confirm Duo MFA fires during Entra ID auth (unified flow).

steps.title: Configure AD sync (if applicable)
steps.detail: If the client has on-premises AD, install Keeper AD Bridge on a domain-joined server for user provisioning.

steps.title: Configure role-based access and shared folder structure
steps.detail: Set up Keeper roles, teams, and shared folder hierarchy. Apply least-privilege access controls.

steps.title: Set master password and security policy requirements
steps.detail: Configure password strength, 2FA enforcement, session timeout, and device approval in Keeper Admin Console → Policies.

steps.title: Invite and onboard primary admin user
steps.detail: Send Keeper invitation to the designated admin user. Walk them through vault setup.

steps.title: Verify SA admin delegate access to enterprise node
steps.detail: Confirm the SA admin account has visibility into the client node and shared folders.

steps.title: Add client domain to EasyDMARC
steps.detail: Log into EasyDMARC. Add the client domain and follow the DNS wizard to generate SPF, DKIM, and DMARC records.

steps.title: Publish SPF, DKIM, and DMARC records at client DNS host
steps.detail: Add TXT records at the client's registrar or DNS host. Document existing records first. Allow 24-48h propagation.

steps.title: Verify DNS propagation in EasyDMARC dashboard
steps.detail: Confirm all records show valid status in EasyDMARC after propagation.

steps.title: Set initial DMARC policy to monitoring (p=none)
steps.detail: Start with p=none. Review report data for 1-2 weeks before tightening to p=quarantine or p=reject.

steps.title: Provision client domain in Mail Protect portal
steps.detail: Log into emailservice.io admin portal. Add the client domain and configure inbound filtering.

steps.title: Update MX records to point to Mail Protect
steps.detail: Update MX records at the client DNS host to emailservice.io values. Document old MX records first.

steps.title: Verify mail flow through Mail Protect
steps.detail: Send a test email. Confirm it appears in Mail Protect logs and delivers correctly.

steps.title: Configure spam and content filter policies
steps.detail: Set up quarantine notifications, allow/block lists, and content filtering rules.

steps.title: Enable Bracket — link rewriting
steps.detail: If the client is opting into Bracket, enable link rewriting in the Mail Protect portal settings for the domain.

steps.title: Enable CloudFilter — cloud app filtering
steps.detail: If the client is opting into CloudFilter, configure and enable cloud application filtering in the portal.

steps.title: Enable SafeSend — outbound DLP
steps.detail: If the client is opting into SafeSend, enable and configure outbound mail policy rules in the portal.

steps.title: Create client child tenant in ControlOne portal
steps.detail: Log into the ControlOne partner portal under the SA parent account. Create a new child tenant for the client.

steps.title: Configure cloud firewall policies for the tenant
steps.detail: Apply the SA baseline firewall policy to the new tenant. Adjust for client-specific requirements.

steps.title: Set up hardware gateway (if client has physical device)
steps.detail: If the client has a ControlOne hardware gateway, claim and configure it in the portal.

steps.title: Configure access groups and user permissions
steps.detail: Set up user groups and remote access policies for the client's user base and SA tech access.

steps.title: Create client account in Syncrify server
steps.detail: Log into the Syncrify admin console. Create a new user account for the client. Set storage quota per agreement.

steps.title: Define backup retention and quota policy
steps.detail: Document the agreed retention period and storage quota. Configure in Syncrify admin and note in the client record.

steps.title: Plan and configure VPN for backup connectivity
steps.detail: Syncrify backups require a site-to-site VPN between the client network and the SA backup network. Select the client's VPN type below.

steps.title: Confirm all org-level configurations are complete
steps.detail: Review each product's portal. Confirm all org-level steps are complete before beginning device deployments.

steps.title: Document all credentials and account details
steps.detail: Store all API keys, passwords, bypass codes, break-glass accounts, and portal login details securely in the client record.

steps.title: Set calendar reminders for deferred follow-up tasks
steps.detail: Threatlocker learning mode review, DMARC tightening, Huntress initial report review, Duo CA policy expansion.

steps.title: Brief account manager on onboarding status
steps.detail: Notify the assigned account manager that org-level onboarding is complete and device deployment is ready to begin.

device_steps.title: Install Syncro RMM agent
device_steps.detail: Use the installer URL stored for this client if available, otherwise obtain from Syncro portal or flash drive script. Run as Administrator.

device_steps.title: Verify agent check-in and assign to correct folder
device_steps.detail: Confirm device appears in Syncro under the correct client. Move it to the Workstations or Servers folder so it inherits the correct policy.

device_steps.title: Deploy Threatlocker agent via Syncro policy
device_steps.detail: The Syncro policy script should trigger Threatlocker install automatically on enrollment. Verify the device appears in the Threatlocker portal in learning mode.

device_steps.title: Deploy Huntress EDR agent via Syncro policy
device_steps.detail: Verify the host appears in the Huntress partner portal under the client org with Active status after the policy script runs.

device_steps.title: Deploy ControlOne agent via Syncro policy
device_steps.detail: Verify the device appears online in the ControlOne management portal after the policy script runs.

device_steps.title: Deploy Keeper desktop application via Syncro policy
device_steps.detail: Verify install on endpoint. Confirm browser extension enforcement policies are applied (HKLM\Software\Policies). Check Chrome/Edge for managed extension indicator.

device_steps.title: Deploy Duo Windows Logon agent (if required for this device)
device_steps.detail: Only if local Windows login protection is required. Run the Duo Windows Logon script from Syncro with ikey, skey, and api_hostname. Test Windows login to verify Duo prompt appears.

device_steps.title: Install and configure Syncrify client (servers only)
device_steps.detail: Install the Syncrify client as Administrator. Connect to the SA Syncrify server using the client account credentials. Configure backup paths, schedule, and retention.

device_steps.title: Verify VPN connectivity to SA backup network
device_steps.detail: Confirm the site-to-site VPN tunnel is active and the SA backup server is reachable from the server.

device_steps.title: Run initial backup — verify completion — perform test restore
device_steps.detail: Trigger first backup manually. Monitor to completion. Verify success in Syncrify dashboard. Restore a sample file to confirm integrity.
